findings.summary: StageResult dataclass is split across two modules with overlapping fields, blurring ownership
evidence: checkpoint.py defines StageResult with fields: name, exit_code, duration, timestamp, completed, status

pipeline_reporter.py imports StageResult from checkpoint and uses it in PipelineReport, but also constructs StageResult instances with only a subset of fields (name, exit_code, duration, status) — the timestamp and completed fields are not populated in this path
pipeline.py imports StageResult from checkpoint and also converts PipelineStageResult -> StageResult, creating a second translation site
findings.suggestion: Move StageResult into a shared models module (e.g. infrastructure/core/models.py) with a clear canonical field set, and eliminate the dual-population paths.

findings.summary: pipeline_reporter.py accumulates report generation, markdown formatting, HTML rendering, error summary, and validation report — four distinct responsibilities in one file
evidence: generate_pipeline_report(), save_pipeline_report(), generate_html_report(), generate_validation_markdown(), generate_validation_report(), generate_performance_report(), save_error_summary(), generate_error_markdown() all live in one 534-line file
HTML report generation embeds ~150 lines of inline CSS and HTML template strings alongside report logic
Validation reporting (generate_validation_report, generate_validation_markdown) is unrelated to pipeline stage reporting but colocated
findings.suggestion: Extract HTML generation to html_templates.py (already exists in reporting/), and move validation report generation to a dedicated validation_reporter.py.

findings.summary: PipelineExecutor._execute_stage double-logs stage start: once via log_pipeline_stage_with_eta and again immediately inside log_operation context manager
evidence: Lines 217-222: log_pipeline_stage_with_eta(...) logs the stage header
Line 225: with log_operation(f'Stage {stage_num}: {stage_name}') logs 'Starting: Stage N: ...' immediately after
Both fire on every stage invocation, producing redundant consecutive log lines at the boundary between caller and callee
findings.suggestion: Remove the log_operation wrapper from _execute_stage since stage-level logging is already handled by log_pipeline_stage_with_eta; or invert and use only log_operation for timing.

findings.summary: generate_pipeline_report accepts stage_results as list[dict] but PipelineReport.stages stores list[StageResult] — the dict-to-dataclass translation is implicit and fragile
evidence: Function signature: generate_pipeline_report(stage_results: list[dict[str, Any]], ...)
Internally converts with: status = 'passed' if result.get('exit_code', 1) == 0 else 'failed' — default of 1 means a missing key silently becomes 'failed'
Callers must know to pass dicts with keys 'name', 'exit_code', 'duration' — this contract is not enforced by the type system
findings.suggestion: Accept list[PipelineStageResult] directly and translate at the single boundary in the caller (pipeline.py), keeping the seam typed end-to-end.

findings.summary: run_pytest_stream has redundant break-condition checks that make the loop termination logic hard to follow
evidence: Lines 120-126: two consecutive if-blocks both check `if process.poll() is not None: break` on the `raw_chunk is None` and `not raw_chunk` branches respectively — these cases could be unified
Lines 143-145: a third `if process.poll() is not None: break` in the else branch duplicates the same termination check
The select loop has three separate exit paths for process completion, making the termination logic non-obvious
findings.suggestion: Restructure the loop with a single explicit sentinel check at the top of each iteration, or use a helper that reads until EOF and returns when the process exits.

findings.summary: get_logger has conditional reconfiguration logic that mixes test-environment detection with propagation enforcement in a way that is hard to reason about
evidence: Lines 173-193: get_logger checks if logger.handlers is empty, then separately checks is_test_env and logger.propagate, then may clear handlers and call setup_logger — three different early-return/reconfiguration paths
The non-test path (lines 190-192) sets propagate=False on every call to get_logger for an already-configured logger, even though setup_logger already sets it
Comment at line 186 explains the rationale but the fix is applied in get_logger rather than enforced once in setup_logger
findings.suggestion: Move the propagate=False enforcement into setup_logger and keep get_logger as a simple cache-or-create with a single code path.

findings.summary: PipelineExecutor mixes pipeline orchestration, log file lifecycle management, and subprocess environment construction in a single class
evidence: _setup_log_file_handler(), _flush_log_handlers(), _verify_log_file() — three methods solely concerned with log file lifecycle, unrelated to stage orchestration
_run_script() builds the subprocess environment (PYTHONPATH, MPLBACKEND, PYTHONIOENCODING, PROJECT_ROOT) — environment assembly is a distinct concern from stage dispatch
The constructor calls _setup_log_file_handler(), establishing a side effect on the filesystem at instantiation time
findings.suggestion: Extract log lifecycle management into a PipelineLogger helper and environment construction into a build_stage_env() function, leaving PipelineExecutor focused on stage dispatch and checkpoint management.

findings.summary: StagePerformanceTracker.end_stage assigns memory_mb and end_memory_mb to the same value, suggesting dead or unfinished design
evidence: Lines 244-245: `memory_mb = current_memory` then `end_memory_mb = current_memory` — both always equal
The metrics dict then stores both keys (lines 261, 262), doubling memory reporting with identical values
start_memory is captured in start_stage but never used in end_stage to compute a delta
findings.suggestion: Replace memory_mb with peak_delta_mb = end_memory - start_memory to surface meaningful information, and remove the redundant end_memory_mb key.
